commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=014c9c6a96b742fff4e9b5f7fcc5494613deac81)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a known-extension correction map and applies it through the existing malformed XML argument cleanup path for agent file-tool parameters.

PR surface: Source +30. Total +30 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the execution-side gap: current main and `v2026.6.9` leave a supplied `report.docodex` value unchanged through the XML-only cleanup helper. The intermittent model-side generation of `.docodex` was not live-reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to choose the canonical correction versus rejection semantics across the open sibling PRs before this external branch can be safely repaired or landed.

target.title: fix(agents): correct model-hallucinated document extensions in file tool path params
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When models blend "codex" into office-document extensions (e.g. GPT-5.4 via Codex runtime), they produce hallucinated paths like `report.docodex` instead of `report.docx`. The existing agent-tool path normalization pipeline only strips malformed XML `</arg_value>>` suffixes â€” it has no awareness of hallucinated file extensions, so these pass through unchanged and cause file-not-found errors.

Fixes #93326

## Summary

Add `correctHallucinatedFileExtension` to the shared agent-tool path normalization pipeline. Known hallucinated extensions (`.docodex`, `.pptcodex`, `.xlscodex`, etc.) are silently corrected to their real counterparts (`.docx`, `.pptx`, `.xlsx`) before filesystem execution.

The correction reuses the existing `stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffixFromKeys` pipeline â€” no new guard layer, no rejection/retry logic, no behavioral change beyond the extension substitution. Both corrections (XML suffix stripping + hallucinated extension correction) run in sequence within the same normalization pass.

**What changed:**
- `src/agents/agent-tools.params.ts` â€” added `HALLUCINATED_EXTENSION_MAP` constant and `correctHallucinatedFileExtension` function; integrated into `stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffixFromKeys`
- `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` â€” added `correctHallucinatedFileExtension` to the direct `stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffix` call in `wrapToolWorkspaceRootGuardWithOptions`

**What did NOT change (scope boundary):**
- No change to agent-tools.read.ts call sites that already use `stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffixFromKeys` (the correction is integrated there)
- No change to bash-tools.exec.ts (already uses `stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffixFromKeys`)
- No new guard layer, no rejection/retry, no new files

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Model-supplied paths like `report.docodex` pass through the current path-normalization pipeline unchanged, causing file-operation failures.

**Environment tested**:
- Runtime: Node 24.13.1, Linux
- Code analysis path: `src/agents/agent-tools.params.ts` â†’ `correctHallucinatedFileExtension` / `stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffixFromKeys`

**Steps run after the patch**:
```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings proof-93326-before-after.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix**:

BEFORE (main branch â€” bug confirmed):
```
=== BEFORE: main branch behavior ===
  âťŚ BUG CONFIRMED: .docodex passes through â†’ {"path":"report.docodex"}
  âťŚ BUG CONFIRMED: .pptcodex passes through â†’ {"path":"slides.pptcodex"}
  âťŚ BUG CONFIRMED: .xlscodex passes through â†’ {"path":"budget.xlscodex"}
```

AFTER (fix branch â€” all corrected):
```
=== correctHallucinatedFileExtension (standalone) ===
Hallucinated extensions that SHOULD be corrected:
  PASS: report.docodex â†’ report.docx
  PASS: path/to/report.docodex â†’ path/to/report.docx
  PASS: /abs/path/report.docodex â†’ /abs/path/report.docx
  PASS: slides.pptcodex â†’ slides.pptx
  PASS: budget.xlscodex â†’ budget.xlsx
  PASS: report.docxcodex â†’ report.docx

Real extensions that must NOT be modified:
  PASS: report.docx â†’ report.docx
  PASS: slides.pptx â†’ slides.pptx
  PASS: budget.xlsx â†’ budget.xlsx
  PASS: notes.txt â†’ notes.txt

=== stripMalformedXmlArgValueSuffixFromKeys (pipeline integration) ===
  PASS: docodex corrected â†’ {"path":"report.docx"}
  PASS: real docx unchanged â†’ {"path":"report.docx"}
  PASS: XML suffix + docodex both corrected â†’ {"path":"report.docx"}
  PASS: pptcodex corrected â†’ {"path":"slides.pptx"}
  PASS: xlscodex corrected â†’ {"path":"budget.xlsx"}
  PASS: only path corrected, content unchanged â†’ {"path":"report.docx","content":"hello"}

âś… ALL PROOFS PASSED
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Hallucinated extensions are silently corrected in the same normalization pipeline that already strips XML suffixes. Real extensions pass through unchanged.

**What was not tested**: Live Gateway/agent session with GPT-5.4 producing hallucinated extensions (no Codex runtime environment available). The correction was verified against the production path-normalization functions directly.
target.author: zoowh
